title: READABILITY: Clarify deprecation and cleanup status of KEY_PACKAGE_RESPONSE
description: core/src/protocol.rs:39-40 — The constant KEY_PACKAGE_RESPONSE is marked 'Deprecated — now a JsonRpcResponse result rather than a named notification.' This explains the functional change but not the lifecycle: can it be deleted? When was it deprecated? Is it used anywhere for backward compatibility? A reader doesn't know whether this is a pending cleanup task or a permanent code path. Fix: change the comment to 'Deprecated in JSON-RPC 2.0 migration (use GetKeyPackageResult via JsonRpcResponse instead). Safe to remove once all clients updated.' or similar, clarifying cleanup scope.
close_reason: P4 readability/comment items. Not blocking correctness. Deferred — file separately if prioritized.
